preamble: The shift from [k] to [i] is a form of vocalization that typically occurs in consonant clusters or intervocalic positions. In the transition from Latin to French, the velar stop weakened into a palatal glide [j], which eventually merged with the vowel system as [i].

examples.note: The velar stop vocalized into a palatal glide.
English borrowed the vocalized form from Old French.

sources: Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
